method_atom: A combinatorial enumeration technique that counts discrete configurations built by stacking units, encoding the counts as multivariate generating functions in which each variable tracks a class assigned to units by the action of a finite Abelian symmetry group; the generating functions are computed via vertex-operator (transfer-matrix) algebra, with a refinement handling the harder cases. Two differently-structured enumerations attached to a space and to its symmetry-quotient resolution are then shown to have matching generating functions, packaged as a conjectural equivalence between the two decompositions.
object_atom: Three-dimensional Young diagrams (plane partitions) and pyramid partitions whose boxes are coloured according to a finite Abelian subgroup G of SO(3), and the Donaldson-Thomas partition functions of the orbifold C^3/G and of its G-Hilbert scheme crepant resolution.
brief_reason: What carries across is the vertex-operator generating-function enumeration of group-colored discrete stackings plus the quotient/resolution matching of two enumerations; what is domain-specific is the plane/pyramid partitions and the Donaldson-Thomas invariants of the particular orbifold C^3/G.
